studentQuestionArray.content: Pancreat(o)- is the combining form for what type of tissue?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Pancreat(o)- refers to the pancreas, which is a glandular organ.

studentQuestionArray.content: Encephal(o)- is the combining form that refers to which part of the body?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Encephal(o)- relates to the brain.

studentQuestionArray.content: Nephr(o)- is the combining form that refers to which organ system?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Nephr(o)- relates to the kidneys and the urinary system.

studentQuestionArray.content: Phleb(o)- is the combining form that refers to which part of the circulatory system?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Phleb(o)- relates to veins and venous drainage.

studentQuestionArray.content: Pulmon(o)- is associated with which bodily function?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Pulmon(o)- relates to the lungs and breathing.

studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Cephal(o)- relates to the head or cranium.

studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: An(o)- is used in word formation to indicate nerves or neural structures.

studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis: Ophthalm(o)- relates to the eye or vision.
